expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly covers: Hebcal API (Jewish), AlAdhan API (Islamic), and Computus algorithm (Christian) -- all three named correctly

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: Full ObservanceEvent TypeScript interface provided with all fields: id, title, rawName, emoji, tradition, denomination, startDate, endDate, isMultiDay, hebrewName, hijriDate, source

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Response uses Promise.all for parallel Islamic month fetching, not Promise.allSettled. The ARE skill specifies Promise.allSettled for graceful partial failure handling. This is a meaningful gap -- Promise.all fails fast on any single month rejection, while Promise.allSettled continues and returns partial results.

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Hebcal described as 'requires no API key'; AlAdhan described with free endpoint; Computus is client-side algorithm -- all free, no auth required

needs_review: Promise.all vs Promise.allSettled: the with-skill executor used Promise.all instead of Promise.allSettled. This is a real bug -- if one AlAdhan month request fails, Promise.all will throw and discard all 11 other successful months.

suggestions.assertion: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
suggestions.reason: The skill should more prominently highlight Promise.allSettled as a REQUIRED pattern, not just mentioned once. The executor read the skill but still used Promise.all. Suggest adding a WARNING box around the Promise.allSettled requirement.

eval_feedback.overall: 3/4 with-skill is a real miss -- the Promise.allSettled pattern needs to be more prominent in the skill body to prevent this failure mode.
